publicationStmt.publisher: "Maryland, Register of Wills Records, 1629-1999." Images. FamilySearch. http://FamilySearch.org: 3 December 2024. Citing Prerogative Court. Hall of Records, Annapolis.

div.head: James Bodkin. Will.
div.p:
In the name of God amen I James Bodkin of Charles County in
province of Maryland Merchant one of the Executors of Dominick Bodkin
of Galloway in the Kingdom of Ireland, merchant although sick and
weak of body yet of perfect and sound minde and memory praise be therefore
given to almighty God do maake and ordaine this my last will and testament
in manner and form following that is to say first and princeipally I comment
my soule into the hands of almighty God hoping the meritts death and passions
of my saviour Jesus Christ to have fill and thee pardon and foregiveness of all
my sinns and to inherit everlasting life and my body I commit to the earth
to be decently buried at the discression of my Executor hereafter named and
touching the distinction of all such worldy Estate as it hath pleased
almighty God to bestowe on me I give and bestow thereof as followeth

First I will that my Debts and funeral charges shall be paid and discharged
thereas by right of my Executor s-- of the aforesaid Dominick Bodkin I--
possessed with a will at the head of Westwood C-- in Charles County and
concerned with mu deceased until the aforesaid Dominick Bodkin in his late
voiage that if he should -- the said will at my own proper cost and charge
paying until them thirty thousand pounds of tobacco and ship there clear of
all charge consigned unto Mr Francis Chapling Hon. merchants in Bruges
in
in Flanders by which contract I am p-- of the said will in my own
right which said contract being accomplished I do give and bequeath
al my tight title and interest in the said will unto my loving brother
Dominick Bodkin now resident in the Island of Antegae and his heirs
forever and in case that my brith at the time of my decease be not
living that then I do give and bequeath the said will unto my
loving brother Edward Bodkin late resident in Galloway in Ireland
and to his heirs forever and in case that my two brothers be not living
at the time of my decease that then I give and bequeath the said will
unto my younger brother Augustin Bodkin and to his heirs forever and
in case at the time of my deacease all my said brothers should be deceased
without I -- that then I do and bequeath the said will unto my lov.
cosen Andrew Bodkin son and heir of my deceased uncle Dominick Bodkin
all my right title and interest in the said will and his heirs forever
he the said Andrew paying unto my loving sisters Ann Jane and Elenor
Bodkin each twenty pound sterling and in case any of my sisters should
decease before they are married my will and desire is that the legacy so
given be paid unto my Honoured father Patrick Bodkin and in case
that my honoured father be at such time deceased that there the said legacy
be paid unto my honored mother Ononias Bodkin.

Whereas I have aright and title to a tract of land by grant from the right
honorable Lord pro-- lying in St. Marys County called Charles Borough
Swamp containing three hundred acres as also one other tract of land lying
in Charles County containing two hundred and fifty acres which said
tract of land I do give and bequeath unto my lo. brother Patrick Bodkin
and his heirs forever and in case that my brother at the time of my
decease be not living that then I do and bequeath the said two tracts
of land unto my borther Edward Bodkin and his heirs forever and
in case
in case my foresaid brother be not living at the time of my decease I--
I do bequeath all the said two tracts of land unto my younger brother
Augustin Bodkin and his heirs forever and in case all my brothers should
be deceased at the time of my decease without issue then I do give and bequeath
the said two tracts of lands unto my cousin Andrew Bodkin and his heirs forever
the aforesaid tracts of land of tehe two hundred and fifty acres lying in Charles County
called Saturday Word.
Item. I give unto mayo William Boarman my plate bullons

Item. I give and bequeath unto Mr. Michael Foster Mr. Thomas Gan-- Mr Francis
Prinington and Mr. Thomas Mapey? each four hundre pounds of tobacco.
Item. I give and bequeath unto Mr. Richard Hobart six hundred pounds of tobacco

I do give and bequeath unto loving cousin James Bodkin six holland shirts two
Dawles shirts four laced cravatts and my crop sorell horse saddle & furniture

Item. I give and bequeath into Charles Quigley my sword and belt and all the
remainder of my waring apparell linens and woolin and -- blade

Item. My will and desire is the said Quigley be allowed five thousand
pounds of tobacco for his years sallary according to agreement deducting
thereout what he stands indebted unto me by book or otherwise and my will
is that all my servants wages be duly paid according to agreement and my
will and desire is that whatsoever debts I have contracted in this province
for the recovery of the said will of the amendment tehreof or otherwise
to happen that there be not afects enought to pay them then my will is that
my Executor hereafter named bind -- the said will unto some honest
ingenius person for some term of years such as my Executr shall
approve of for the satisfaction of my debts and thatby creditors may be
allowed reasonable consideration for the time of the for baranee of this debts
item.

Item. I do give and bequeath unto Mary Boarman wife of Maj. William
Boarman eight hundred pounds of tobacco to buy her a ring.

Item I give and bequeath unto Thomasin Gibbins servant of mayo. William
Boarman two hundred pounds of tobacco.

Item. I give and bequeath unto Jane Philips my servant woman a young
black heifer one breeding sow.

Item. I do give and bequeath unto James Robinson servant to D-- Patrick J--
two hundred pounds of tobacco Item I do give and bequeath unto James
Ellis of St Marys County four hundred pounds of tobacco.

Item. I do and bequeath unto Robert Carich one fowling piece and
I do nominate and declare and appoint and do hereby request Mr. Clement Hill and Mr. Robert Carich both of St. Marys County to be Executors of this
my last will and testament and I do hereby rewoke dis-- and make
void all former wills and testaments by me heretofore made In witness
whereof to this my last will and testament I have set my hand and off-
my seal the twenty third day of August Anno Domino 1683.

Sgned sealed and published
In the presence of us
Patrick Sines Francis Green
Thomas Clark
James Bodkin

On the back of the aforegoing was the following Endorseent (to wet)
This day appeased before me Clement Hill and Robert Carich of St. Marys
Gen. Ex. of the last will and testamnet of James Bodkin Gen dec. at
Newton in the St. Marys County and took the oath of Ex. and duly administarted
the said Estate of the dec. according to Law and to -- Inventory
in this marks as witness my hand this twelth day of December
H Digges
January 8th Anno 1683 Then come before me under writtem Francis Green and
Thomas Clark and made oath that the within will the set and deed
of James Bodkin and that they see him the within mentioned Bodkin sign
and seal the samee as witness my hand the day and year -- above
written
12 1/2 Sides
Jo. Pi--
